title: Battle of Geroon
content:
{{Top|canon=Unidentified battle (Vagaari)}} {{Conjecture|legends}} {{Battle |prev=[[Battle between the Chiss and Yuuzhan Vong]][[Outbound Flight (novel)|''Outbound Flight'']] |conc= |next=[[Destruction of Special Task Force One]] |image=[[File:Chvfighter.jpg]] |name=Battle of Geroon |conflict=[[Outbound Flight Project]] |date=[[27 BBY/Legends|27 BBY]][[Outbound Flight (novel)|''Outbound Flight'']] takes place [[Standard year/Legends|five years]] after the [[Battle of Naboo/Legends|Battle of Naboo]], which ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates to [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]], therefore, the events of ''Outbound Flight'' must take place in [[27 BBY/Legends|27 BBY]]. As the events of the novel take place in the same time period, the Destruction of Special Task Force One takes place before the Battle of Outbound Flight |place=[[Geroon]] |result=Enslavement of the [[Geroon (species)|Geroon]] [[Species/Legends|species]] and theft of a [[Vagaari/Legends|Vagaari]] [[Gravity well projector/Legends|gravity well projector]] |side1=[[Geroon government]] |side2=[[Vagaari Empire]] |side3=[[Chiss Ascendancy/Legends|Chiss Ascendancy]] |side4= |commanders1=Unknown |commanders2=A [[Unidentified Miskara (Jorj Car'das)|Miskara]] |commanders3=[[Force Commander]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Mitth'raw'nuruodo]] |commanders4= |unit1= |unit2= |unit3=*[[Chiss Expansionary Fleet/Legends|Chiss Expansionary Fleet]] **[[Picket Force Two/Legends|Picket Force Two]] |unit4= |forces1=*100+ Geroon fighters |forces2=*100+ [[Vagaari warship]]s and [[Vagaari starfighter|Vagaari fighter]]s *100 [[Transport/Legends|Vagaari civilian transports]] *6 [[Gravity well projector/Legends|Vagaari ship net]]s |forces3=*''[[Springhawk/Legends|Springhawk]]'' *6 [[Starfighter/Legends|Chiss fighters]] |forces4= |casual1=Nearly all Geroon defenders |casual2=*Unknown number of Vagaari *At least 3 Vagaari warships heavily damaged *Many Vagaari fighters *1 projector captured by the Chiss |casual3=*Damage to ''Springhawk'' *Mitth'raw'nuruodo wounded *3 Chiss crew members killed |casual4= |civilian= }} {{Quote|Interesting, I seem to have miscalculated."
"No kidding, let's get out of here before someone sees us."
"No, you misunderstand. I knew the battle would be of this scale. What I hadn't realized was the Vagaari's true nature. You see those other vessels?"
"The ones waiting their turn to fight?"
"They're not here to fight. Those are the civilians. The error I spoke of was that the Vagaari aren't simply a strong, well-organized pirate force. They're a completely nomadic species.|Thrawn and Jorj Car'das upon arriving at the scene of the battle|}} The [[Vagaari/Legends|Vagaari]] attempted to enslave and plunder the [[Geroon (species)|Geroons]] by '''attacking''' their [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of [[Geroon]]. [[Force Commander]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn]] used possibly one of his favorite tactics, the [[Thrawn Pincer]] here. Thrawn stole the [[Gravity well projector/Legends|Vagaari ship net]], a primitive version of the [[Gravity well projector/Legends|Gravity well projector]], but was injured in the battle. ==Background== The Vagaari fleet, numbering over 100 warships, attacked the [[Geroon]] homeworld. The [[Geroon (species)|Geroons]] put up a fight, but they were too outnumbered. Force Commander Thrawn investigated the battle and discovered that the Vagaari had made a practical gravity well projector. He noticed that one of them was relatively unprotected with three war vessels, and sought to steal it. His purpose was two-fold, to obtain this useful technology for the [[Chiss/Legends|Chiss]], and to provoke the Vagaari into attacking the Chiss, in which retaliation would be justified. [[Jorj Car'das]] and [[Maris Ferasi]], two captives from the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]], accompanied Thrawn on the ''[[Springhawk/Legends|Springhawk]]''. ==The attack== Thrawn ordered his fighters to make a hyperspace jump toward the gravity projecter depending on the gravity well to pull them out when they got close. He then told them to clear out enemy fighters and create a defensive perimeter between the projector and the war vessels. The ''Springhawk'' followed when the fighters had been eliminated. Thrawn ordered Assault Teams [[Assault Team One|One]] and [[Assault Team Two|Two]] to board the projector and neutralize the operational crew while [[Chief engineer/Legends|Chief Engineer]] [[Yal'avi'kema]] and three of his crew tried to find a way to collapse the projector to take it aboard or attach it to their [[Hull/Legends|hull]] for transport. Soon the boarders had eliminated the Vagaari crew and were working on collapsing the projector. Car'das noticed there were [[Zero-g plastic bubble]]s on the ships and when Thrawn called for a close up they realized that a slave was held in each of the bubbles. Thrawn knew he could do nothing about it, however, and he ordered his fighters to fire full [[Missile/Legends|missile]] bursts and [[Conner net|shock nets]] at all three warships, the shock nets aimed at the vessels' [[Bridge (starship)/Legends|bridges]] and command stations. The gravity projector was spark-welded to the ''Springhawk''{{'s}} hull and they were ready to jump into hyperspace. However, before they could jump, a Vagaari attack hit the bridge. Thrawn was injured and thrown to the floor by the blast. Maris and Car'das tried to put a [[sealant patch]] on a tear in his [[Environment suit/Legends|vacc suit]], but it would not bond to the material. Maris was forced to lie chest-to chest over him pressing her stomach against the wound until two Chiss brought a larger sealant patch and carried him away. The ''[[Springhawk/Legends|Springhawk]]'' made the jump to hyperspace. ==Aftermath== The Vagaari defeated the remaining Geroons, and began to pillage the planet. The Chiss lost two warriors, which had later consequences as the raid was seen by some as overt aggression. Thrawn soon recovered at the [[Crustai asteroid base|Crustai base]]. The theft of the gravity well projector would make a difference in later battles, and the techniques that Thrawn developed from this helped secure him many victories. ==Appearances== *[[Outbound Flight (novel)|''Outbound Flight'']] {{Ab|Outbound Flight (unabridged audiobook)}} {{1st|in book}} *''[[Choices of One]]'' {{Imo}} *''[[Survivor's Quest]]'' {{Ab|Survivor's Quest (unabridged audiobook)}} {{1stm|in book}} {{Imo}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Schlacht um das Geroon-System |es=Batalla del mundo natal geroon |fi=Geroonien kotiplaneetan taistelu }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Geroon, battle of}} [[Category:Battles in the Unknown Regions]] [[Category:Events during the Outbound Flight Project]]
